note: Bottom-up replacement for the v32 multiplicative damage model that M8 found imbalanced. severity = net + bounded_strength(floor(Str/2) cap 3) - armour_resist(type, tier); decisive vs unarmored (net>=3 wounds directly); reach = -1 Ob closing, NOT damage. NO crit doubling, NO Str/weapon multiplier. Class A = retained canonical anchors (net-as-severity, weapon-type taxonomy). Class C = PROPOSED redesign (Jordan-authorized, NOT canonical, sim-tunable): bounded-Str cap, per-type ARMOR_RESIST (plate negates cut / weak vs blunt), decisive threshold, reach Ob. Self-test 7/7 = unit-level HISTORICAL validation (plate negates cuts; blunt+thrust defeat plate; strength bounded edge not multiplier; unarmoured lethal; reach=tempo; armour monotonic). Historical grounding: half-sword/estoc (thrust-vs-plate), poleaxe/warhammer (blunt-vs-plate), Liechtenauer/Fiore (skill/measure over force), 'spear is king' (reach). Does NOT edit canon; presented as proposal for Jordan ratification.

entries.quoted_text: net hits (margin of success) is the primary wound-severity input; degree bands 0/1/2/3+
weapon damage type from reach/weight/type: Blade vs Blunt; thrust-primary vs cut-primary (combat_v30 §8.1)

Strength contributes additively and BOUNDED: floor(Str/2), capped — replaces multiplicative Str x weapon-mult. Historical: a stronger arm helps but does not multiply lethality; placement/edge/target dominate.
Strength bonus hard cap (floor(Str/2) capped at 3, i.e. Str>=6 gives +3). Prevents the Str-7 runaway that M8 surfaced; keeps the strong-vs-skilled contest historical.

Per-type wound resistance by armor tier (severity subtractor). Bottom-up from historical arms-and-armor: plate (heavy) NEGATES cuts (resist 4 ~ defeats all but exceptional hits), is moderate vs thrust (resist 2 — thrust-to-gaps, estoc/half-sword), and WEAK vs blunt (resist 1 — concussive trauma transmits through plate; poleaxe/warhammer were the armor-defeating weapons). Light/medium scale down. Magnitudes proposed, sim-tunable.

Against an UNARMORED target a clean hit (net>=3) inflicts a wound directly rather than chip severity — models historical lethality of solid blows to soft targets (fights are short and decisive, not attritional). Threshold proposed, sim-tunable.

Longer reach grants the opener an Ob advantage (effective -1 Ob to the longer weapon while closing) rather than a damage bonus. Historical: 'the spear is king' — reach controls the measure and the first tempo, not the wound size. Proposed, sim-tunable.
